genes.nl_summary: This strategy outlines how to leverage Linux cgroup 'cpuset' to dedicate specific CPU cores and memory nodes to critical containers, ensuring predictable performance and mitigating 'noisy neighbor' issues.

payload.summary: This strategy outlines how to leverage Linux cgroup 'cpuset' to dedicate specific CPU cores and memory nodes to critical containers, ensuring predictable performance and mitigating 'noisy neighbor' issues.
strategy: Identify target container processes and available CPU/NUMA resources.
Create a new dedicated cgroup under the 'cpuset' subsystem.
Initialize the new cpuset cgroup with parent's CPU and memory node settings.
Configure the 'cpuset.cpus' and 'cpuset.mems' files for the cgroup with specific CPU cores and memory nodes.
Assign the target container processes (or start new containers with appropriate flags) to this newly configured cpuset cgroup.
